entry.title: LIVE SESSIONS on SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps – Data & AI Content Updates
entry.content:
Our next LIVE SESSION, in collaboration with the SAP Global User Group Organization, will take place June 09, 2026, at 17:00 (CET). Join us to hear from Michael and Mirna who will take you through the recent SAP Enterprise Support updates including:

  • SAP Datasphere in Business Data Cloud: CQC Deployment Readiness and CQC Solution Optimization
  • SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-guided Implementation Workshop Series
  • SAP Business AI & Joule: Expert-guided Implementation Workshop Series

 

 

➡️Join live or watch the replay: LIVE SESSIONS on SAP Enterprise Support value maps – Data & AI Content Updates

❓In the live session, you will have the opportunity to ask questions in Q&A. You will find more details in these links:

  • Ensuring a Smooth SAP Datasphere Deployment: The Value of Continuous Quality Checks
    • Continuous Quality Check Info Sheet: CQC-for-Deployment-Readiness_Datasphere.pdf
    • Continuous Quality Check Info Sheet: CQC-for-Solution-Optimization_Datasphere.pdf
  • SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-Guided Implementation Workshop Series
  • New Expert-Guided Implementation: Unlocking Joule and SAP Business AI from Strategy to Activation
  • New Expert-Guided Implementation: Joule Prerequisites and Activation

Note: Access to the Expert-guided Implementation (EGIs) require a one-time registration to SAP Learning

 

SAP Enterprise Support

Providing targeted guidance and self-service content, SAP Enterprise Support is part of the Foundational Success Plan – included with every SAP cloud solution. Content and services include self-paced learning materials, workshop-style interactive remote sessions and continuous quality check (CQC) remote delivered services. Leverage the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps to get advice on the appropriate SAP Enterprise Support content and services for your needs.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps

SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps — your go-to team for Foundational Success Plan guidance and enablement — have a dedicated SAP Community Group. Watch our short demo video, and go to our page to explore our range of topics. To access our Community Group, sign in with your S-user ID or Universal ID. Within the group:

  • Select Data and Analytics Value Map
  • Select SAP Business AI, Clean Core & Sustainability
  • Click “Join Group” and “Subscribe” to stay updated
  • Request a call with an SAP Enterprise Support topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals

 

About the author: 
Caroline Sheridan is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in SAP Data and Analytics.

💡Have questions or feedback on this content? Feel free to leave a comment below or contact @Caroline-S

Step 03: In background, an In House repair number generates, from where we do Receive from Customer (RE2 order is created) - In-Bound delivery is created and when PGR is done 657 movement.

entry.title: SAP University Alliances Academic Board established for EMEA region
entry.content:
🚀 A Historic Milestone for Academia–SAP Collaboration Across EMEA
We are proud to announce the official launch of the SAP University Alliances Academic Board – EMEA, established on 1st May 2026.
The new EMEA Board complements other regional Academic Boards worldwide and aims to become a strategic bridge between academia and the SAP ecosystem — connecting research, innovation, talent development, and digitally enabled organisations across one of the most diverse regions in the world.
It represents an extraordinary academic ecosystem with SAP University Alliances partnerships involving: 🏛️ 850+ universities, 🎓 200,000+ students, from 🌐 ~50 countries across Europe, the Middle East and Africa.
 
🎯 Strategic priorities of the Board include:
🤖 Developing future-ready curricula integrating AI, Analytics, and other digital and innovative enterprise technologies
🎓 Strengthening talent pipelines and employability across the SAP ecosystem
🔬 Promoting research collaboration, pedagogical innovation, and international partnerships
🤝 Enhancing collaboration between universities, SAP, partners, and customers
The inaugural Academic Board includes 19 members representing more than 15 countries, with Jorge Carrola Rodrigues, PhD as President, Sanjeeb Mohanty (NED, CITP, GCIO, SFHEA) as Vice President, and the Founding Members Andrea Matta, Dr Andy Callaghan, Charlotte Casteele, Edouard de BOYSSON, Enas Musa Allozi /PhD, FHEA, Gianluca Mattarocci, Linda Badri Khalfa, Miloš Ulman, Mike van Roosmalen, Rafael Monterde-Diaz, Selçuk Kıran, Sotiris Gayialis, Stephany Rajeh, Tamas Orosz, Viorel-Costin Banta, PhD, Abbey Littler, Holger Wittges
This marks the start of a globally connected, future-focused academic ecosystem for SAP across EMEA. 🚀

Follow also the LinkedIn page https://www.linkedin.com/company/116067160

entry.title: FB05 transfer posting with clearing does not update WITH_ITEM for withholding tax at payment
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am facing an issue related to Extended Withholding Tax in Brazil scenario.

Context:

  • IRF category configured as “withholding tax at payment”

  • Vendor invoice posted successfully

  • IRF information exists correctly in vendor master/customizing

  • Process uses transfer posting with clearing (FB05)

Issue:
When executing FB05 for transfer posting with clearing, the withholding tax is not recalculated and table WITH_ITEM is not updated.

System displays message:
7Q332 – “Withholding tax information is not considered”

I already checked SAP Notes explaining that:

  • FB05 / clearing transactions are generally not payment-relevant

  • WITH_ITEM is not updated in non-payment relevant events

  • Only payment-relevant processes (F110, F-53, FBZ2, FB05 incoming/outgoing payment) generate withholding tax items

However, in this case the business requirement is specifically for IRF configured “at payment”.

My questions are:

  1. Is there any standard way to force withholding tax calculation during FB05 transfer posting with clearing?

  2. Is there any BTE/BAdI/enhancement recommended by SAP for this scenario?

  3. Has anyone implemented a custom solution to generate/update WITH_ITEM during clearing?

  4. For IRF at payment, is SAP recommendation always to use payment transactions instead of clearing transactions?

Additional context:

  • Brazil localization

  • Extended Withholding Tax activated

  • ACM / contract settlement process involved

  • Custom logic currently posts accounting entries manually in POST_FI

Any guidance or best practice would be appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Which BADI is used to add sold to party bp in conditi...
entry.content:
Perform the below steps

  1. Create a custom logic implementation in the Custom Logic app (or via classic BAdI in on‑premise).
  2. Use the BAdI (APOC_ADD_CONDITION_PARAMETER or APOC_ADD_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS) to read the sold‑to party from the sales document header partners.
  3. Populate this value into the OPD context so it can be used in BRF+ decision tables.
  4. Update your BRF+ decision table to include the new condition column “Sold‑to Party.”
  5. Test by creating a sales order and checking if the output determination now evaluates the sold‑to party.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Dimension Tables Move to the File Repository
entry.content:
Introduction

Dimension tables in SAP Analytics Cloud are moving to the file repository. This completes the journey that started with stories, models, data actions, multi actions and currency conversion tables — and it brings with it a significantly simpler approach to privilege management, a cleaner user experience, and a unified way to organize all your planning artifacts in one place.

In this post we walk through what is changing, what the new experience looks like, and what you need to know about the central migration that will move your existing dimensions automatically. This enhancement is shipped with the QRC3.2026 upgrade on QRC and preview systems and with wave 2026.11 on fast-track tenants.

 

Why This Matters: Key Benefits

All in one place

Dimension tables are the final object type to make the move. With this release, stories, models, data actions, currency conversions, and dimension tables all live in the file repository and follow the same handling paradigm. There is no longer a separate mental model for dimensions — everything works the same way.

Greatly Simplified Privilege Maintenance

Managing access to dimensions has historically been one of the more painful aspects of SAP Analytics Cloud administration. The new approach eliminates that friction in several important ways.

  • There is no longer any need to maintain roles specifically to hide a dimension that contains sensitive data. File repository sharing handles this naturally.
  • When a new dimension is created in "My Files", safe defaults apply automatically: only the creator has full control, plus administrators with access to private user folders. No one else can see the new dimension until it is explicitly shared.
  • You can configure access on a folder once and then simply move dimensions into that folder — they automatically inherit the folder's privileges. This is a significant time saver for tenants with many dimensions and complex access requirements.
  • A long-standing pain point is also resolved: the role maintenance UI used to display every individual dimension, including hundreds or thousands of "Date" and "Version" dimensions. This made it nearly impossible to identify such specific dimensions and, in tenants with large numbers of dimensions, caused the UI to become slow due to missing lazy loading. Individual dimensions no longer appear in the role maintenance UI at all.

 

What Changes in the User Interface

Public Dimensions in the File Repository

Public dimensions are now visible in the file repository browser alongside models, stories, and other artefacts. You can rename them, move them, update their descriptions, or transfer ownership — all without affecting any models that reference them.

Sharing Instead of Role Maintenance

To change privileges for a dimension or grant access to other users or teams, you now use the standard file repository sharing functionality. Individual dimensions no longer appear in the role maintenance UI.

Public Dimension Tables Landing Page

The public dimension table landing page no longer loads and displays all public dimensions on open. Instead, it shows the 25 most recently used public dimension tables — consistent with the behavior already in place for models and currency conversions.

Saving a New Public Dimension

When saving a new public dimension, the file repository save dialog appears, allowing you to specify the parent folder, the name, and a description — exactly as you would when saving any other file repository artifact.

Creating Dimensions Inside a Model

It is no longer possible to choose "Make Public" when adding a new dimension directly inside a model. The alternatives are:

  • Create public dimensions upfront before creating the model and reference them when building the model.
  • Create a new private dimension, save the model, then use "Save As Public Dimension" to create a copy and "Dimension Table > Replace" to swap the Private dimension for the newly saved public one.

New Create Dimension Dialog

The "Create Dimension" dialog has been redesigned. It now includes a file repository browser for selecting existing public dimensions of a given type — Account, Organization, or Generic. In seamless planning models, it only allows picking dimensions saved in the same SAP Datasphere space as the model.

Replace Dimension Dialog

It now includes the same file repository browser for selecting existing public dimensions.

Private Dimension Tables

Private dimension tables do not appear in the file repository browser. However, access management has moved away from roles and uses file repository functionality:

  • You now control who can see and edit a private dimension using the same file repository sharing mechanisms available for all other artifacts, reachable directly from the model or dimension editor. Sharing a model with a user or team automatically shares all private dimensions of that model with the same privileges.
  • It is also possible to restrict access to individual private dimensions further than the model itself.

 

Central Migration: What Happens to Your Existing Dimension Tables

The migration to the file repository runs automatically as part of the QRC3.2026 upgrade on QRC and preview tenants or as part of the wave 2026.11 upgrade on fast-track tenants. Here is what you need to know.

What Gets Migrated

  • All public dimension tables receive a file repository artefact.
  • These artefacts are created in a new folder: Public > Dimension Tables.
  • Private dimensions are technically migrated as well but they will not be visible in the file repository.

What does not change

Note that the migration does not change the dimension tables itself. IDs and content stay the same. No effects on the consumption in models, stories, data actions etc. are to be expected.

How Existing Role Assignments Are Handled

The migration applies the same algorithm used for earlier file repository migrations such as Data Actions, Multi Actions and Currency Conversions.

  • Teams following the naming convention "DimensionRole_<role name>" are generated for roles where at least two users have that role directly assigned. Those users are added to the generated team, and the relevant dimensions are shared to that team. For roles with single user or only team(s), the permissions will be attached to the individual or team(s).
  • If the tenant already has all users organized in teams with roles assigned to those teams, no additional teams will be generated — existing teams are reused.

Privilege gaps after migration

Due to the way file repository privileges work, some users may find they are missing access to dimensions after the migration.

  • Before the migration, a create-only role was sufficient — users automatically had full control over any dimension they created as the owner. The file repository does not work this way.
  • To achieve the same level of control as before, users need a role that explicitly grants Read, Delete, Update, and Maintain privileges.
  • We recommend reviewing your role assignments after the migration and updating them accordingly.

Import of Transport Packages

When importing transport packages created before the migration, dimension tables are automatically saved/migrated to the file repository.

Bookmarks to Public Dimension Tables

Browser bookmarks created to directly access public dimension tables will cease to work after the migration. We need to add a prefix for all public dimension tables to avoid name collisions with other objects.

Behavior During the Migration Window

While the migration is running, creating and deleting dimensions is blocked and will result in backend errors. Depending on the number of dimensions and roles in your tenant, this could take several hours.

The migration runs right after the upgrade of your tenant to QRC3/wave 2026.11. This means users can be logged in while the migration is in progress and may encounter these backend errors during that window.

 

Summary

The move of dimension tables to the file repository is a significant step forward. It completes the unification of all object types under a single, consistent artefact management model, eliminates long-standing pain points in role maintenance, and gives administrators a much more intuitive and scalable way to control access to sensitive data. We recommend reviewing your role assignments after the migration and updating them accordingly.

entry.title: Get Early Insights to SAP AI Innovations: Join SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 by June 19
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire, SAP reaffirmed its strong commitment to AI-driven innovation and its role in transforming businesses.

Innovation at SAP is built on collaboration. Leveraging cutting-edge technologies like Artificial Intelligence, SAP continues to drive meaningful business value for customers. Now, we invite you to be part of this journey by participating in SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects focused on AI.

🗓Registration is open until June 19, 2026.

In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, early involvement is key. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can collaborate directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing feedback on concepts, prototypes, and innovations while they are still taking shape.

View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the selected exciting opportunities:

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-Assisted Error Remediation for SAP Application Interface Framework
  • AI assisted guidance in the UI theme designer
  • BTP Specific Data Privacy & AI based Data Masking
  • Fulfillment of business needs through Business Adaption Plans and Solution Implementation
  • Partner Perspectives in SAP BTP and AI development

Cloud ERP

  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)

SAP Globalization

  • AI for UI: Exploring the Usage of AI in Translating SAP's Product User Interface
  • Assessing Demand for AI Speech Services in SAP Translation Hub

SAP Concur

  • Concur Invoice - AI-first, Reimagine Invoice Experience

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative meets SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Hands-on: AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Mgmt - Provisioning & Setup
  • Hands-on Experience of AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Management
  • Hands-on System Experience of the AI-Assisted Process Modeler in SAP Signavio

Others

  • Autonomous Supply Chain: Embodied AI in the Warehouse
  • Embodied AI Autonomous Asset Inspection
  • Embodied AI: Autonomous Visual Inspection for Quality and Safety
  • Future of SAP Product UX
  • Providing feedback on the Discovery domain in SAP LeanIX

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: How to add a new entity to the RAP BO of a customizing table
entry.content:
@internet_kccrm 

Besides the association to the parent you also define an association to the root. D is the entity, C the parent and All the root

"BDEF entity D:
define behavior for I_SmbcTableD alias SmbcTableD ##UNMAPPED_FIELD
lock dependent by _SmbcTableAAll
authorization dependent by _SmbcTableAAll

association _SmbcTableAAll { with draft; }
association _SmbcTableC { with draft; }

"CDS view entity D
define view entity I_SMBCTABLED
  as select from smbc_d
  association [1..1] to I_SmbcTableA_S      as _SmbcTableAAll on  $projection.SingletonID = _SmbcTableAAll.SingletonID
  association        to parent I_SmbcTableC as _SmbcTableC    on  $projection.KeyA = _SmbcTableC.KeyA
                                                              and $projection.KeyC = _SmbcTableC.KeyC

entry.title: Re: Metadata update has been deferred issue in SSAM
entry.content:
When you mention "create metadata folder - include custom application in this folder" does this mean your metadata includes two sub-directories?  One for the base SSAM metadata and a second directory for your customizations?

If this is the case then you need to make sure you have updated the MDKProject.json to include the "BaseProject" property pointing to the name of the directory under metadata containing the SSAM metadata.

entry.title: Re: Building an AI Agent for SAP Maintenance using MCP, CAP and Claude Desktop
entry.content:
Hi @venkatamandavilli

Thanks for the feedback,

You nailed the core idea. I kept the proposal intentionally simple as a proof of concept, but in a real enterprise environment you'd definitely need to layer in security, governance, monitoring, and more robust integrations.

What's exciting is that in the last few days several new announcements around AI agents and SAP integrations have started popping up. I believe SAP will deliver more native tools and frameworks over the next months, which should make this kind of implementation much more accessible for customers.

Thanks again for the great insights and discussion!

ENG: 

At SAP Labs Latin America, our Campus Strategy initiatives aim to connect universities and students with the technology market through academic visits, talks, hackathons, and talent development programs. We work with institutions across Latin America to create opportunities that bring academia and industry closer together, helping students gain practical experiences, connect with professionals, and explore career opportunities at SAP.  If your institution is interested in organizing academic visits, partnership initiatives, or SAP talks, feel free to contact us:

Nathália Rauzer — nathalia.rauzer@sap.com

Trabalhamos com instituições em toda a América Latina para criar oportunidades que aproximem a academia e a indústria, ajudando estudantes a terem experiências práticas, se conectarem com profissionais e explorarem oportunidades de carreira na SAP.

entry.title: Re: Lemur maps integration with SSAM
entry.content: Hi Muhammad, Thanks for your response. When the user creates a Notification pin and navigate to SSAM, will this entry record in traces or SSAM log - I mean the data entered by the user in map. Here we have a repeat Notification feature - so when the user identifies an issue , he is presented with a screen on map which contains user details like customer contact..etc. Now when user clicks on create Notification on Map he is navigated to SSAM where he enters notification details..etc. Now when the user wanted to raise an issue on the map for the same customer/FL he will be presented with repeat Notification button on map when the user clicks on that he is navigated to SSAM and the data he entered previously will be copied to SSAM notification create page. Now here few values are not copied since first Notification is not sync, we cannot check the values in traces and I could not find in SSAM logs as well . How can we check what values that are passed from Lemur to SSAM when user clicks on repeat notification - where can we check these logs Thank you. Regards, Sravanthi

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Journal entry category in sales order process
entry.content:
See kba 3060075 - Journal Entries without Entry View 

which outlines the reason for no entry view.

See referenced therein 

  • - Entry View Summarization 

The Activate Entry View Summarization configuration activity includes the following options:
...

Suppress If Possible

For journal entries that don't contain any accounts that use open item management (for example, customer or supplier items) or any tax or tax-relevant items, and that aren't manually posted using the Post General Journal Entries app, you can choose to hide the entry view completely. If you select this option, no entry view is displayed for goods movements (that is, goods issues, stock transfers, etc.), for example.

  • Documents posted with Journal Entry Attribute U are not displayed within app Display Line Item Entry (App ID F2218). They can be displayed in app Display Line Items in General Ledger (App ID F2217)

  • A change of the posted Documents with status 'U' is not possible. 

  • It is not possible to edit assignment/attachments for Journal Entries with "Journal Entry Attribute" U (Posting in General Ledger Only)

  • A change is only possible for Journal Entries which have an entry view. Example Supplier/Customer Invoice.

 

Note "Suppress if Possible" is the default setting in newly installed tenants.

entry.title: Re: Special G/L Indicators - Need provision to create new...
entry.content:
Hello!

 The creation or modification of Special G/L Indicators in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is classified as non-approved expert configuration and is restricted under standard system behaviour. This is formally documented by SAP in KBA 2761212 (Create or Change Special G/L Indicator in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition), which is the primary reference for this topic (link here: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2761212 )

This restriction exists because SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition operates on a shared multi-tenant architecture where SAP manages and controls core configuration to ensure system stability, upgrade compatibility, and compliance across all customers on the platform. Unlike Private Edition or On-Premise deployments, the Public Cloud edition intentionally limits certain customizations to preserve these qualities.

While creating new Special G/L Indicators or renaming existing ones is not permitted as a self-service activity, SAP does provide a functional workaround for the most common business need, mapping transactions to specific G/L accounts. There is a configuration called "Automatic Account Determination" where you can define alternative reconciliation accounts for Special G/L transactions. This means that even though the indicator code and description remain as delivered by SAP, you can redirect the posting to a company-specific G/L account that aligns with your Chart of Accounts and reporting structure (e.g., separate accounts for advance payments, security deposits, or retention amounts). You can adjust the reconciliation account for existing Special G/L Indicators through the Automatic Account Determination configuration, This is the recommended and supported approach within Public Cloud.

For cases where the standard indicators genuinely do not meet a business requirement, there is a formal exception process: The creation of a new Special G/L Indicator is a non-standard expert configuration. This is a customer-specific request and needs to go through the review process for non-standard configuration.  Approval is not guaranteed; SAP Product Management reviews each request. Details here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/steps-to-take-for-non-approved-expert-configuration/qaq-p/12326580

 Additional links with details:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2807810

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/40a00cc64416482a97beab4399142791.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Routing Principle: in SAP CPI with Value Mapping metadata ODCP in pract
entry.content:
@venkatamandavilli 

Thank you for the thoughtful feedback — and for articulating exactly the right diagnosis.

You identified the real failure point: not the tooling, but the absence of a governance contract that separates what is stable from what must change. CPI is capable. The problem is almost always architectural — mappings scattered, routing logic embedded in iFlows, no clear ownership boundary between layers.

The Immutable Routing Principle is not a CPI trick. It is a direct consequence of ODCP — Orchestration Domain-Centric Pattern, Layer 3 of SDIA (Semantic Domain Integration Architecture). The invariant is simple:

The artifact owns the contract. The metadata owns the variability.

In practice:
— The iFlow never changes because it owns nothing that should change.
— The Value Mapping changes because it owns everything that must change.
— The domain boundary is explicit — not implied, not scattered across iFlows.

What you described in your own projects — broken mappings, non-centralized routing, unpredictable behavior for downstream systems — is what SDIA calls Naming Chaos and Package Sprawl. They are not accidents. They are the natural outcome of building integration without a governance and the DOMAIN LAYER that defines where variability lives.

ODCP enforces that boundary by design. One package per domain. Every artifact carries the domain token. Routing metadata is the contract — versioned, centralized, and owned explicitly.

The same principle runs at every layer of the architecture:
— API Management: the proxy never changes. The KVM grows.
— SAP CPI IDoc: the iFlow never changes. The Value Mapping grows.
— SAP CPI Kafka: the adapter never changes. The Value Mapping grows.

Same invariant. Three layers. One architecture.

Full specifications if you want to go deeper:
SDIA — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18877635
ODCP — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18876593
SDIA Ecosystem — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19004802

entry.title: Clarification on Project Billing Process, Use of Invoice Cancellation vs Credit Memo
entry.content:
1-What is the correct standard procedure when an invoice must be fully reversed: is an invoice cancellation + rejection of the initial PBR/BDR + new PBR/new invoice sufficient, or should a credit memo also be created and sent to the customer in this scenario?

2-What are the standard differences between invoice cancellation and credit memo in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, in terms of FI postings and project impact (do both create separate FI documents without deleting the original invoice, and do both affect project analytics)?

3-Is it possible to use separate output templates for invoices, invoice cancellations and credit memos (different header labels such as “Facture d’annulation” / “Avoir”, negative amounts, reference to the original invoice), and how should this be configured in Output Management?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Project Billing Process, Use of Invo...
entry.content:
Hello Customer,

Related to your 3 questions, kindly check the answers below:

  1. Invoice cancellation creates a separate reversal document that reverses the accounting impact while preserving the original document in the system. The cancellation process automatically generates reversed accounting documents and creates entries for tax compliance. 

    Credit memos create new corrective documents that reference the original invoice, allowing for partial corrections and maintaining clear business relationships between documents. Both approaches ensure that original documents remain traceable for audit purposes. 

  2. There are no differences between cancelling credit memo and F2 invoice (billing document), you cancel both in the same application: 
    1. Access the Manage Billing Documents app 
    2. Search for the billing documents you want to cancel
    3. Select the billing document and choose Cancel 
    You might refer to: Canceling Billing Documents

  3. Yes, SAP provides separate standard form templates for different billing document types. For example, in various country localizations, you'll find distinct templates:

    • Customer Invoice Templates: SDBIL_CI_STANDARD_XX (where XX is country code)
    • Credit Memo Templates: SDBIL_CM_STANDARD_XX 

    These templates are designed specifically for their respective document types and include appropriate legal text, formatting, and data presentation requirements. If you would like to customize and create your own, you might follow our documentation: 

    2679030 - Extensibility for Billing Document Printout - SAP S/4HANA Cloud

    How to Extend a Custom Form Template for Billing Output with Custom Fields

    2984817 - Form design in Adobe LiveCycle Designer

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 8 license doesn't allow subreport man...
entry.content:
WOW that's going back a few years...

The issue you are running into is due to in CR 8 there was an RCAPI (Report Creation API) keycode that enabled developers to use that ability. I believe it was an extra expense....

Without an RCAPI keycode there is no way to enable the ability, it's throttled within the craxdrt.dll itself.

By the way it was recommended to upgrade CR 8 to CR 8.5 for licensing and other permissions etc.

To install it required installing CR 8 on the DEV PC so it generated the proper registry key.

Sorry but there is no way to enable it without that keycode.

Only recommendation I can give is to upgrade to the current CR .NET and Visual Studio 2022 or VS 2025. There is no RDC Engine available now and to replace it you can use the CR .NET ReportClientDocument to allow RCAPI functions to be used. You basically have to create your own RCAPI functions using .NET. No 1 to 1 API functions to map using .NET.

You can get the Installer to VS from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html?locale=en-US&ai=true

There are 2 sample app's on that page to assist getting started:

And refer to the Licensing info for deploying internal or external applications:

Important Notes:

  • Please refer to Knowledge Base article 2007224 for more info on configuring WEB Applications in Visual Studio 2013.
  • Manual deployment of crdb_xml drivers requires an installed JVM
    • 1871962 – How To manually deploy Service Pack 6, and above, crdb_XML data source driver for Crystal Reports Developer for Visual Studio
      • Note: Clickonce32/64 is managed by the xml file for which one is installed.
    • 1534388– CRVS2010 – Creating Click Once deployment
      • Note: Click Once “Homesite” is to tell your installer to go here for the runtime.

Related Content

Related Documents

  • Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Licensing
  • Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages for instructions on upgrading your existing project to the latest SP
  • See these blogs on how to use Crystal Reports for Visual Studio sample applications:
    • How to use the new CR 2020 export to Microsoft Excel(XLXS) in Visual Studio .NET code (new)
    • How To: Parameters in Crystal Reports for Visual Studio .NET
    • Printing Crystal Reports in .NET
  • SAP Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Installation Guide ( SP 21 and above)

Good luck

entry.title: Re: Lemur maps integration with SSAM
entry.content: Since "Repeat Notification" is handled by the Lemur native extension internally and there's no SAM-side logging for it, your options are:MDK Client Log — Enable trace-level logging in the MDK client settings (Settings > Log Level > Trace). The binding data passed to the callback rule should appear in the client log when the action fires.Add temporary logging — In NotificationCreateFromMap.js or NotificationCreateChangeSetNav.js, add a Logger.info call to dump JSON.stringify(context.binding) — this will show exactly what properties Lemur passes to SAM when "Repeat Notification" is tapped. Lemur-side logs — The Map extension itself may have its own logging. Check with the Lemur team if they have a way to enable verbose logging to see what data it stores from the first notification and replays on the repeat action.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Two Tenants with different FYV (4-4...
entry.content:
Hello!

 Fiscal Year Variant K4 (calendar year, Jan–Dec, 12 periods) and SZ / 4-4-5 do not share the same period start and end dates. This creates a mismatch when trying to consolidate financial data at the group level.

Group reporting in this scenario supports:

- Period mapping / consolidation period assignment. Group Reporting works with its own consolidation periods (independent of the source system's FYV). When pulling data from subsidiary ledgers via the Data Monitor, the system maps source periods to consolidation periods. However, this mapping works cleanly only when period boundaries align between the source FYVs and the consolidation calendar.

- Multiple Source Ledgers / Systems. Group Reporting can assimilate data from multiple source systems (relevant to your two-system proposal). Each entity (consolidation unit) can theoretically come from a different system with a different FYV.

- Manual Data Entry / Upload. For entities whose FYV doesn't align with the group calendar, data can be entered or uploaded manually into the consolidation layer after period-end restatement.

 Group reporting don’t supports:

- SAP Group Reporting does not automatically prorate or restate financial figures when two FYVs have different period. There is no built-in engine to say "SZ Period 3 partially maps to K4 Period 2 and partially to K4 Period 3."

- The automated journal entry or balance carryforward pull from S/4HANA source systems into Group Reporting relies on matching period keys. If the SZ system's period 3 ends on a different date than K4 period 3, the data collection mapping will not produce a meaningful 1-to-1 comparison at reporting periods.

- If both K4 and SZ happen to share the same fiscal year-end date ( both end December 31), annual consolidation may be feasible. However, quarterly or monthly consolidation would remain problematic.

 

Recommendations will be to define the group reporting consolidation calendar based on one FYV (typically K4 / calendar year) and require the Real Estate entities using SZ to submit restated figures on a calendar-period basis for consolidation purposes.

Another approach is to use manual upload for the SZ Entities. The SZ based entities can close their books on their 4-4-5 calendar and then upload period-adjusted balances into Group Reporting using the manual data entry or file upload method, mapped to the group's K4 consolidation periods.

If the customer only consolidates annually and both FYVs share a December 31 year end, the two system approach with annual Group Reporting consolidation is workable with moderate manual effort.

entry.title: Urgent SAP Support Ticket – MIRO Invoice Line Item Limitation in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are facing a critical issue during Supplier Invoice posting (MIRO / Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Currently, the system allows summarization only up to approximately “999” line items in the entry view, after which the system throws the error:

  • “Maximum number of items reached in entry view”
  • Along with invoice verification issues during posting.

However, our business process requires booking supplier invoices with more than DIGITS line items in a single invoice document.

Due to this standard system limitation:

  • Business users are unable to complete invoice posting
  • Invoice processing is getting delayed/on hold
  • This is impacting regular business operations and month-end processing

We request SAP to:

  1. Confirm whether this is a standard SAP limitation in Public Cloud
  2. Provide immediate resolution/workaround
  3. Advise whether the line item limit can be increased through configuration or SAP note implementation
  4. Suggest any alternative standard process for handling high-volume invoice postings


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  • Open app:
    • “Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced” (MIRO)
  • Enter the following details:
    • Supplier/Vendor
    • Company Code
    • Invoice Date
    • Reference Document Number
  • Select Purchase Order reference
  • Load a Purchase Order containing high-volume line items
    • Example: More than 999 line items
    • Business scenario requires around DIGITS+ line items in a single invoice
  • Select all PO items for invoice posting
  • Simulate/Post the invoice
  • System throws the following messages:
    • “Maximum number of items reached in entry view”
    • Multiple “Price too high (tolerance limit exceeded)” messages may also appear
  • Invoice document cannot be processed completely due to system limitation




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Urgent SAP Support Ticket – MIRO Invoice Line Item Li...
entry.content: When creating a Supplier Invoice, similar to other processes, the underlying FI document Entry View includes a 3-digit item counter. This counter imposes a limit on the number of items allowed per document. To address this limitation and reduce the count of line items in the Entry View, See KBA 0002845836 - Error F5727 "Maximum number of items in FI reached" in relation to GR/IR clearing accounts` and links referenced therein.

entry.title: Re: S4 Hana Cloud AP & FICA open items netting
entry.content:
Hello!

FICA operates as a high-volume subledger that transfers aggregated totals to the General Ledger per reconciliation key during each posting run, not individual open items. Because of this, FICA items do not exist as individual FI open items at the Business Partner level, which is the prerequisite for standard AR/AP netting.

Standard BP netting (linking Customer and Supplier roles on the BP master) requires both the receivable and the payable to be individual open items in the Universal Journal. FICA items are not posted this way, so automatic clearing and manual clearing cannot offset them against AP items.

In  S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition following standard approaches are available:

- Structured manual clearing at period-end: this is the applicable operational workaround within standard Public Edition scope. Use the FICA balance report to determine the net FICA balance per Business Partner. Review FI-AP open items for the same BP via the Manage Supplier Line Items. Post a manual offset/clearing document via the Post General Journal Entries app  to reflect the net position on the balance sheet. Include this step in the period-end closing checklist with a named process owner.

- Net balance reporting view: regardless of the clearing approach, a reporting-layer solution should be implemented to provide visibility over the net AP/FICA position per Business Partner. Use standard Fiori analytical apps to report on both AP open items and FICA balances side by side. This supports Trial Balance review, audit requirements, and management reporting without requiring any additional configuration. This is achievable through the standard reporting tools available in Public Edition.

Direct automatic netting between AP and FICA open items at the Business Partner level is not possible in standard S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition without SAP delivering this as a dedicated Scope Item. The manual process above is the supported path until SAP provides a standard solution.

entry.title: Re: Cannot see YCOA Change Log in SAP Prod
entry.content:
Hello!

This is confirmed standard behaviour in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, documented in SAP KBA 3664740, https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3664740

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3748845

Where to view the change history (old and new values)

- View change logs in the quality / configuration system.  This is the correct and intended place to see full change history with old and new field values. Use the "View Logs" or "Change History" option within the app to see all changes made, including old and new values, user, and timestamp. This is where the authoritative change record lives for all YCOA changes transported to Production.

- Use the "Display G/L Account Changes" app. You can use the app "Display G/L Account Changes" to see changes on an account, including if the Account Group was changed. This app provides field-level change tracking and is available in the system where the change was recorded (Q system).

 In the Production system, the "Synchronize G/L Account Master Data" app only shows current changes, not a full historical change record as maintained in the Test/Q system. Full history is maintained only in the originating system.  This means:

Production → shows who ran the Synchronize app or which transport was applied, but not the field-level old/new values.

Quality system → shows full field-level change history with old value, new value, user, and date.

For audit and governance purposes, all YCOA change reviews and approvals are conducted in the Q system before transport.

there are generally two ways to set up custom fields:

  1. You can start in the App Custom Fields.
  2. You can start in the application you want to enhance.

I usually prefer using option 2 because I find it easier to create the field exactly where I want it.

 

To use option 2, in your customizing system you need to open the WBS Element, then navigate to Adapt UI.

In UI Adaptation mode, you can then choose the position.

Once you click on "Add: Field" you will be able to create the custom field in that exact position.

entry.title: Re: Multiple Cash Clearing Accounts with Single Account S...
entry.content:
Hello!

 First question: 

Account symbols use a masking mechanism to derive the GL account. For example, if the house bank GL account is 1000000 and the clearing account is 1000001, the account symbol mask would be set up as +++++++++1 — meaning the system takes the house bank's GL account and replaces the last character with "1".

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/automatic-supplier-payment-clearing-in-s-4hana-public-cloud/ba-p/12829129

Applied to this scenario:

House bank JPDP1/1EG2 is assigned to GL account 11001011

Account symbol mask +++++++080 takes the base GL account 11001011 and replaces the last 3 characters with 080, deriving 11001080.

The root cause of the error is a GL account range mismatch: the clearing account 11002080 sits in a different number range (11002xxx) than the house bank main account (11001xxx). The masking logic cannot bridge different GL account ranges using a single symbol,  it can only modify the suffix of the same base number.

To resolve this, exist two options:

Option 1: Align the clearing account number to the same base range as the house bank GL. If the main account is 11001011, create the clearing account as 11001080 and assign it correctly in GL master data as a Bank Subaccount linked to the reconciliation account. The mask +++++++080 will then resolve correctly.

Option 2: Define a dedicated account symbol with a fixed GL account (11002080 hardcoded, no masking) and assign it explicitly to the posting rule for this house bank. This removes the dynamic derivation entirely for this account but avoids renumbering.

 

Second question:  

A single account symbol with a mask cannot map different house bank accounts to different clearing accounts if those clearing accounts are in different GL number ranges. The mask applies uniformly across all house banks using that symbol.

When the bank reconciliation account is activated for the house bank account, the bank subaccount is derived by the account symbol. You must ensure the bank subaccount derived by the account symbol is properly created in GL account master data and assigned to the correct bank reconciliation account. Details here:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3376166

 For multiple house banks each requiring a different clearing account, the correct approach is:

Create separate account symbols per house bank clearing account (e.g. JPCLEAR1, JPCLEAR2), each with a fixed or appropriately masked GL account.Assign each account symbol to the relevant posting rule and external transaction type combination for the respective house bank. This avoids relying on a single mask to serve multiple incompatible GL ranges.

 

Third question:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, posting rules are predefined and preconfigured and cannot be altered by customers. These rules are standardized to ensure consistency and compliance across the platform.  Creating a new posting rule is considered non-standard expert configuration. Details here: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3357476

The posting rule requires two posting areas to achieve both the GL posting and the AR subledger clearing simultaneously:

Posting Area 1 - posts to the bank GL account (debit bank, credit clearing account)

Posting Area 2 - clears the AR open item in the subledger

A second line must be added to the posting rule with Posting Area 2 (Subledger Accounting).  If Posting Area 2 is missing from the posting rule, the system posts to GL but does not trigger AR clearing.

The option for bank statement automation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is the use of Processing Rules. Details here: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3374309

Processing Rules allow you to define matching logic (e.g. match bank statement line to open AR item by reference number, payment order number, or amount) without modifying locked posting rules.

 

References:

 SAP KBA 3376166 — House bank not assigned to GL account (FINS_ACDOC_POST133)

SAP KBA 3374309 — Posting and processing rules in bank statement posting — S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP KBA 3357476 — Creation of posting rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP KBA 2854415 — Information on bank statement posting rules in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Credit Management for Professional Services Project B...
entry.content:
Hello!

There is no standard, direct integration between SAP Credit Management (FSCM) and the Project Billing process in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition at the Project Billing Request or Billing Document Request (BDR) level. The configuration activity "Activate Credit Management in Service Transactions" founded is specific to Service Management objects (service orders, service contracts) and does not apply to Professional services customer projects (scope items J11 / 4E9).

In the Professional Services scenario, there is always a 1:1 relationship between the Sales Order and the Project. The Sales Order is automatically created when the project stage is set to "contract preparation". The WBS billing element ID is stored in the sales order item. SAP Learning: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sales-intercompany-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/introducing-project-billing

Closest approach using standard:

Step 1. Activate FSCM credit management with sales order integration. 

Configure Credit Check rules: static check (open receivables + open orders) and/or overdue items check.

Assign the credit check to the relevant sales document types used by your customer projects.

Set the reaction to block the sales order (credit status set to "blocked") when the customer exceeds the limit or has overdue items above threshold.

Step 2: When a credit block is set on the sales order, the Billing Specialist working in "Manage Project Billing" will still be able to see the billing elements, but when attempting to release the BDR and convert it to a final invoice, the system will block the billing because the sales order item is credit-blocked. The credit block on the sales order effectively prevents the final invoice from being posted.

Step 3: Credit Release process. From a master data point of view, the Business Partner role UKM000 must be created for each relevant customer. Credit limit and credit segment data are maintained in the Business Partner master under the SAP Credit Management role. Credit managers can review and release blocked documents via the "My Documented Credit Decisions" Fiori app (equivalent of UKM_MY_DCDS). SAP Learning: https://learning.sap.com/courses/functions-innovations-in-sap-s-4hana-sales/working-with-sap-credit-management_d6b177ee-a8bb-4b77-80dc-87c04a9dd3f1

There are two important limitations compared to your original requirement:

  • The credit block is placed when the sales order is created or when a credit re-evaluation runs, not when the billing specialist processes the project billing request. The billing app itself does not trigger a real-time credit check. This means a customer could become overdue after the sales order is created, and the billing would not be automatically re-blocked.
  • The "Manage Project Billing" app does not provide the credit block. The billing specialist may not receive a meaningful error at the billing request step. The block becomes visible when attempting to create the final invoice/billing document, not upstream in the project billing workflow.

Overall configuration proposal:

Activate FSCM credit management and configure overdue item check and credit limit check for the sales document type used in your customer projects.

Schedule the credit re-evaluation job (available in "Schedule Credit Management Jobs" app) to run nightly or on a defined frequency, so that customers who become overdue after Sales Order creation are re-evaluated and blocked.

Define a credit release workflow so that the credit/finance team is notified and can approve or release blocked billing.

As a process control, add a credit status check to the period-end billing checklist so the billing specialist verifies there are no credit-blocked sales orders before releasing BDRs.

entry.title: SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Kafka Topic Routing Mechanism Using SDIA with ODCP for SAP CPI
entry.content:
"The iflow kafka topic routing  per domain is immutable. The metadata is the architecture."

Introduction

If you have worked with SAP PI/PO or even with SAP CPI IDoc routing, you already know the pattern: one receiver, one router, metadata drives the decision. The artifact never changes — the configuration does.

This blog will support you about IDOC:

SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Routing Principle: in SAP CPI with Value Mapping metadata ODCP in practice

The same principle applies to Kafka but when teams start integrating Kafka topics into SAP CPI, the most common approach creates one iFlow per topic, one deployment per event type, one change request per new business event. A Sales O2C domain with 5 event types becomes 5 iFlows, 5 deployments, and 5 maintenance points. When the topic naming changes or a new event is added — a developer is required.

This post presents a pattern that eliminates that overhead. One Kafka Sender Adapter. One router iFlow. One Value Mapping that grows. All iFlows inside the domain package are immutable.

This is not a workaround. This is ODCP applied to event-driven architecture in SAP CPI over the Domain Layer that lives above the tecnology.

The pattern was validated end-to-end with:

  • Confluent Cloud free tier as the Kafka broker
  • SAP BTP Integration Suite (CPI) standard Kafka Sender Adapter — no custom adapter, no additional cost
  • AVRO binary deserialization via Groovy Script using Apache Avro 1.12.1
  • Three input scenarios validated: raw bytes (production), Base64, and Hex (simulation/testing)
  • Confluent Wire Format detection (magic byte 0x00 + schema ID) handled automatically

⚠ This pattern is designed within the SDIA domain-centric governance framework using ODCP semantic name convention contract. If your organisation does not follow SDIA/ODCP, adapt the package names, iFlow names, and VM artifact names to your convention. The routing logic and Groovy script work independently of naming.

⚠ Scope Notice

The implementation demonstrated in this blog represents a foundational template — a minimal, working proof of concept designed to illustrate the immutability principle applied to Kafka topic routing in SAP CPI.

Schema design, field mapping, error handling strategy, retry policy, DLQ configuration, security hardening, and output format are intentionally kept simple. Every adaptation required for your specific landscape — domain model, event contract, operational SLA — is left to the implementing team.

The intent of this blog is not to deliver a production-ready solution. The intent is to demonstrate that one Kafka Sender Adapter, one router iFlow, and one Value Mapping are sufficient to implement a fully governed, domain-scoped, immutable routing mechanism in SAP CPI — without a single line of hardcode and without a custom adapter.

The principle is the deliverable. The implementation is your decision.

What is SDIA and why does it matter here ?

SDIA (Semantic Domain Integration Architecture) is a vendor-agnostic governance model built on one invariant: the business domain is the primary key of every artifact, from the API gateway to the data layer.

ODCP (Orchestration Domain-Centric Pattern) is Layer 3 of SDIA and governs SAP BTP Integration Suite directly for SAP CI - Cloud Integration — one package per domain and subdomain ( other possible levels of granulality ), every artifact carries the domain token in a deterministic name, credentials segmented and etc..

In this pattern, the Kafka router iFlow is the domain boundary entry point — immutable, owned by the domain team, zero shared dependencies. The Value Mapping is the metadata contract. The processing iFlows are independently owned and deployed.

The same invariant applies at every layer of SDIA:

API Management  → GDCR/DDCR → KVM grows.     Proxy never changes.
SAP CPI IDoc    → ODCP      → VM grows.      iFlow never changes.
SAP CPI Kafka   → ODCP      → VM grows.      iFlow never changes.

The Architecture

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║             SDIA — Kafka Topic Domain Routing Architecture                       ║
║             Pattern: ODCP · Layer 3 · Sales O2C Domain                           ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
 LAYER 1 — KAFKA RECEIVER LAYER (Immutable · Zero Hardcode · Static iFlow)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

 Package : acme.sales.o2c.integrations
 iFlow   : id00.o2c.kafka.topic.routing.f.in.async
 VM      : id00.o2c.vm.metadata

 ┌──────────────────────────────────┐
 │         Confluent Cloud          │
 │                                  │
 │  acme.sales.o2c.order.created    │
 │  acme.sales.o2c.order.confirmed  │
 │  acme.sales.o2c.order.cancelled  │──────▶  Kafka Sender Adapter
 │  acme.sales.o2c.invoice.created  │         (all topics, one adapter)
 │  acme.sales.o2c.*                │
 └──────────────────────────────────┘
                                               │
                                               ▼
                               ┌───────────────────────────────┐
                               │         processData()         │
                               │                               │
                               │  reads kafka_receivedTopic    │
                               │  from message header          │
                               │                               │
                               │  key: acme.sales.o2c          │
                               │       .order.created          │
                               │                               │
                               │  VM lookup:                   │
                               │  id00.o2c.vm.metadata         │
                               │                               │
                               │  result: target_channel       │
                               └──────────────┬────────────────┘
                                              │
                                              ▼
                                     ◇─────────────────◇
                                     │     Router      │
                                     ◇─────────────────◇
                                      │               │
                       is_mapped=true─┘               └─is_mapped=false
                      │                                      │
                      ▼                                      ▼
              ┌──────────────┐                     ┌─────────────────────────┐
              │     End      │                     │   buildErrorPayload()   │
              └──────┬───────┘                     │                         │
                     │                             │   kafka_topic           │
                     │ ProcessDirect               │   vm_artifact           │
                     ▼                             │   action_required       │
        ┌────────────────────────┐                 └────────────┬────────────┘
        │   ${target_channel}    │                              ▼
        └────────────┬───────────┘                 ┌────────────────────────┐
                     │                             │      ✗ Error End       │
                     ▼                             │        HTTP 500        │
 ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┐└────────────────────────┘
 │                 acme.sales.o2c                 │
 │ ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
 │ │ order.created   → /id01/.../created        │ │
 │ ├────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │
 │ │ order.confirmed → /id02/.../confirmed      │ │
 │ ├────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │
 │ │ order.cancelled → /id03/.../cancelled      │ │◀─── acme.sales.o2c.order.created
 │ ├────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │
 │ │ invoice.created → /id04/.../invoice-created│ │
 │ └────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
 └───────────────────────────┬────────────────────┘
                             │
                             │ ProcessDirect internal
                             ▼

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
 LAYER 2 — DOMAIN PROCESSING LAYER (SDIA Governed · Independent Ownership)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

 Package : acme.sales.o2c.integrations
 iFlow   : id01.o2c.kafka.order.created  ← template · same pattern for all events
 
 ⚠ This iFlow is a template. The same structure applies to every subsequent
   processing iFlow in the domain — id01.o2c.kafka.order.confirmed,
   id01.o2c.kafka.invoice.created, and so on. Only the business logic inside
   changes. The routing mechanism, the adapter, and the router iFlow
   remain identical and untouched.

 ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │  ProcessDirect   │     │                Domain Processing                 │
 │  local listener  │────▶│                                                  │
 │                  │     │  · AVRO deserialization                          │
 └──────────────────┘     │  · message mapping                               │
                          │  · content enrichment                            │
                          │  · schema validation                             │
                          │  · retry policy    ← domain team defines SLA     │
                          │  · JMS queue       ← domain team defines         │
                          │  · DLQ             ← domain team defines         │
                          │  · alerting        ← domain team defines         │
                          │  · target adapter  (HTTP · SOAP · RFC · OData)   │
                          └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 The Receiver Layer is frozen. The Domain Layer evolves.
 A new event type never touches the router iFlow, 
only the external parameter regards the Domain and Subdomain a new possible topic.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Why This Is Not Overengineering

This question deserves an honest answer — because in some scenarios, it genuinely is.

When it IS overengineering:

If your domain has 2 or 3 stable topics that never change, creating a router with a Value Mapping adds a layer with zero return. One iFlow per topic — simple, direct, maintainable.

When it is NOT overengineering:

When the domain is alive — new business events are added frequently, topic naming evolves, or multiple teams contribute to the same domain. In that scenario:

 
Without router:  New event → new iFlow + new adapter config + new credential + deploy  
With router:  New event → 1 row in Value Mapping → done. Zero deploy. Zero code.

The Value Mapping becomes the live catalog of every event the domain knows and processes. That is not overhead — that is governance.

The question that defines the decision:

"How often do new events appear in this domain?"

Domain volatility Design
Rare — stable topicsDirect adapter per topic
Frequent — growing eventsRouter + VM · immutable iFlow
UnknownRouter + VM · safe by default

The Routing Key — Kafka Topic as Semantic Contract

Unlike IDoc routing where the key is assembled from multiple header fields, Kafka routing is simpler and more elegant. The topic name is the semantic contract. It already carries domain, subdomain, and event intent:

acme.sales.o2c.order.created
  │      │    │    │      │
  │      │    │    │      └── event: what happened
  │      │    │    └───────── detail: which process
  │      │    └────────────── subdomain: which process
  │      └─────────────────── domain: which area
  └────────────────────────── company

One field. One lookup. One result. No composite key assembly needed.

Value Mapping Configuration

Artifact: id00.o2c.vm.metadata

The AVRO Schema — Externalized by Design

The AVRO schema is stored as a CPI Externalized Parameter — not hardcoded inside the Groovy script. This is not optional — it is the only acceptable design:

Hardcoded schema  → schema changes → script change → deployment required
Externalized      → schema changes → update parameter → zero deployment

The iFlow remains immutable. The schema evolves outside it.

Configure under the iFlow Externalized Parameters:

Name  : avro
Value : { "type": "record", "name": "Order", ... }

The Groovy Scripts

Iflow routing Kafka Topic inside the Domain Layer for Sales - O2C - CI Package:

As you can see the adapter Kafka is reading all topics regards Sales - O2C - Process:

Topics:

acme.sales.o2c.order.created,acme.sales.o2c.order.confirmed,acme.sales.o2c.order.cancelled,acme.sales.o2c.invoice.created

Don't forget to add the Allowed Headers: kafka.TOPIC, this is the unic reference for the routing.

Confluence Kafka - Topics - Free Tier:

Create your own Kafka instance in 3 minutes and test it: https://www.confluent.io/get-started/

Script 1 — Router (id00.o2c.kafka.topic.routing.f.in.async)

 

// =============================================================================
//  SAP Community Blog — Draft
//  title   : Domain-Driven Kafka Routing in SAP CPI using Value Mapping
//  author  : Ricardo Viana — SAP BTP Integration Suite Expert
//  version : 1.0.0
//  date    : 2026-05-22
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
//  pattern : SDIA — Semantic Domain Integration Architecture
//  purpose : reads kafka topic header, performs vm lookup, sets target_channel
//            for processdirect routing within the same integration package
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
//  functions exposed:
//    processData(message)       — reads kafka topic, calls vm, sets target_channel
//    buildErrorPayload(message) — replaces body with json error on failure
// =============================================================================

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory
import com.sap.it.api.mapping.ValueMappingApi

// =============================================================================
//  function : processData
// =============================================================================
def Message processData(Message message) {

    def vm_agency_src='sales.o2c.kafka'
    def vm_id_src='kafka.topic'
    def vm_agency_tgt = 'acme.enterprise.division'
    def vm_id_tgt     = 'acme.br.pd.o2c.iflow'
    def fallback      = 'unknown'

    def props   = message.getProperties()
    def headers = message.getHeaders()

    // ── reads kafka topic from header ─────────────────────────────────────────
    def kafka_topic    = (headers['kafka.TOPIC'] as String)?.trim() ?: ''
    def target_channel = fallback
    def vm_error       = ''

    try {
        def vm_api = ITApiFactory.getApi(ValueMappingApi.class, null)

        def mapped = vm_api.getMappedValue(
            vm_agency_src, vm_id_src, kafka_topic,
            vm_agency_tgt, vm_id_tgt
        )

        if (mapped?.trim()) target_channel = mapped.trim()

    } catch (Exception e) {
        vm_error = e.message ?: 'unknown error'
    }

    // ── output properties ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    props.put('target_channel', target_channel)
    props.put('is_mapped',      (target_channel != fallback).toString())
    props.put('vm_key',         kafka_topic)
    props.put('vm_error',       vm_error)
    headers.put('vm_key',       kafka_topic)

    return message
}

// =============================================================================
//  function : buildErrorPayload
// =============================================================================
def Message buildErrorPayload(Message message) {

    def props          = message.getProperties()
    def target_channel = (props['target_channel'] as String)?.trim() ?: 'unknown'
    def kafka_topic    = (props['vm_key']         as String)?.trim() ?: 'n/a'
    def vm_error       = (props['vm_error']        as String)?.trim() ?: 'none'
    def timestamp      = new Date().format("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")

    def error_map = [
        header: [
            script  : 'acme.core.script.kafka.router',
            author  : 'Ricardo Viana - SAP BTP Integration Expert',
            severity: 'error'
        ],
        input: [
            kafka_topic: kafka_topic
        ],
        routing: [
            timestamp     : timestamp,
            vm_key        : kafka_topic,
            target_channel: target_channel,
            is_mapped     : false,
            vm_artifact   : 'acme.kafka.domain',
            vm_error      : vm_error
        ],
        action_required: "Register topic '${kafka_topic}' in value mapping 'acme.kafka.domain'"
    ]

    message.setBody(
        groovy.json.JsonOutput.prettyPrint(
            groovy.json.JsonOutput.toJson(error_map)
        )
    )
    message.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json')

    return message
}

Iflow processing topic inside the package Sales O2C for the topic as sample

Kafka Topic for: acme.sales.o2c.order.created - Iflow to process: id01.o2c.kafka.order.created

Script 2 — Processing Iflow (id01.o2c.kafka.order.created)

I'm going to left to possibilities of output as JSON or XML than you change as per of your needs, the sample I used XML as output because of that JSON is commented.

// =============================================================================
//  SAP Community Blog
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
//  title   : Kafka Topic Routing Mechanism Using SDIA with ODCP for SAP CPI
//            — Domain-Driven Event Routing for Sales O2C Topics
//  author  : Ricardo Viana — SAP BTP Integration Architect | SDIA Creator
//  series  : SDIA — Semantic Domain Integration Architecture
//  pattern : ODCP — Operational Domain Contract Pattern
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
//  abstract:
//    This script demonstrates how to implement a domain-scoped Kafka topic
//    router inside SAP Cloud Integration using the SDIA governance model.
//    The AVRO deserialization is decoupled from the input source — the same
//    logic handles raw bytes from the Kafka Sender Adapter, Base64, or Hex,
//    with only a single line change depending on the scenario.
//    The AVRO schema is externalized via CPI Externalized Parameters,
//    making the iFlow immutable — no code change required when the schema
//    evolves.
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
//  related artifacts:
//    value mapping : acme.kafka.domain / acme.kafka.topic
//    ext parameter : avro (AVRO schema JSON)
//    iflow package : ACME.Sales.O2C
// =============================================================================

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import org.apache.avro.Schema
import org.apache.avro.generic.GenericDatumReader
import org.apache.avro.generic.GenericRecord
import org.apache.avro.io.BinaryDecoder
import org.apache.avro.io.DecoderFactory
import groovy.json.JsonBuilder
import groovy.xml.MarkupBuilder
import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream

def Message processData(Message message) {

    // ── 1. INPUT — choose according to your scenario ──────────────────────────
    //
    //  PRODUCTION (Kafka Sender Adapter — raw bytes):
        byte[] rawBytes = message.getBody(byte[].class)
    //
    //  SIMULATION — Base64 string (e.g. Content Modifier):
    //  byte[] rawBytes =  Base64.getDecoder().decode(message.getBody(String.class).trim())
    //
    //  SIMULATION — Hex string (e.g. Content Modifier):
    //  def hex = message.getBody(String.class).trim().replaceAll("\\s+", "")
    //  byte[] rawBytes = hex.replaceAll("[^0-9a-fA-F]", "").toList().collate(2)
    //                       .collect { it.join() }
    //                       .collect { Integer.parseInt(it, 16) as byte }
    //                       .toArray(new byte[0])
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    // ── Confluent wire format detection ───────────────────────────────────────
    // Confluent prepends: [ 0x00 magic ][ 4 bytes schema ID ][ avro payload ]
    // If detected, strip the first 5 bytes before deserializing.
    byte[] avroBytes
    if (rawBytes[0] == 0x00) {
        avroBytes = rawBytes[5..-1] as byte[]
    } else {
        avroBytes = rawBytes
    }
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    // ── 2. Schema via Externalized Parameter (iFlow immutable) ────────────────
    def schemaStr = message.getProperty("avro").toString()

    // ── 3. Deserialize AVRO → GenericRecord ───────────────────────────────────
    Schema schema = new Schema.Parser().parse(schemaStr)
    ByteArrayInputStream bais = new ByteArrayInputStream(avroBytes)
    BinaryDecoder decoder = DecoderFactory.get().binaryDecoder(bais, null)
    GenericDatumReader reader = new GenericDatumReader(schema)
    GenericRecord decoded = (GenericRecord) reader.read(null, decoder)

    /*// ── 4. GenericRecord → JSON ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    def builder = new JsonBuilder()
    builder {
        orderId     decoded.get("orderId").toString()
        customerId  decoded.get("customerId").toString()
        totalAmount decoded.get("totalAmount")
        currency    decoded.get("currency").toString()
        status      decoded.get("status").toString()
        createdAt   decoded.get("createdAt")
        items       decoded.get("items").collect { item -> [
            sku     : item.get("sku").toString(),
            quantity: item.get("quantity"),
            price   : item.get("price")
        ]}
    }*/
    
    // ── 5. GenericRecord → XML ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    Writer writer = new StringWriter()
    def indentPrinter = new IndentPrinter(writer, ' ')
    def builder = new MarkupBuilder(indentPrinter)

    
    builder.Order {
        orderId     decoded.get("orderId").toString()
        customerId  decoded.get("customerId").toString()
        totalAmount decoded.get("totalAmount")
        currency    decoded.get("currency").toString()
        status      decoded.get("status").toString()
        createdAt   decoded.get("createdAt")
        Items {
            decoded.get("items").each { item ->
                Item {
                    sku      item.get("sku").toString()
                    quantity item.get("quantity")
                    price    item.get("price")
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    message.setBody(writer.toString())
    message.setHeader("Content-Type", "application/xml")

    //message.setBody(builder.toPrettyString())
    //message.setHeader("Content-Type", "application/json")

    return message
}

Required JAR

Upload to iFlow under Resources → Archives (JAR):

 

Library Version Source
avro-1.12.1.jar1.12.1https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/apache/avro/avro/1.12.1/
 

Sample of avro and schema

 Dinary base64 sample input to Kafka Topic

  "value": {
    "type": "BINARY",
    "data": "HE9SRC0yMDI0LTAwMTIzEENVU1QtNDU2AAAAAABzl0AGQlJMAICgq4f0YwQOTUFULTAwMQYAAAAAAOR1QA5NQVQtMDAyAgAAAAAAIHxAAA=="
  }
}

 Avro Schema:

  "fields": [

    },

      "type": "string"

    },

    },
    {

      "type": {
        "type": "enum",
        "name": "OrderStatus",
        "symbols": [
          "CREATED",
          "CONFIRMED",
          "CANCELLED"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "createdAt",

    },
    {
      "name": "items",
      "type": {
        "type": "array",
        "items": {
          "type": "record",
          "name": "OrderItem",
          "fields": [
            {
              "name": "sku",
              "type": "string"
            },
